mission.title: envoy: Feature Request: In lua http filter add ability to set the contents of the body
mission.description: Feature Request: In lua http filter add ability to set the contents of the body. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
_Feature Request_: **In lua http filter add ability to set the contents of the body**

```yaml
filter_chains:
- filters:
  - name: envoy.http_connection_manager
    config:
      http_filters:
        - name: envoy.lua
          config:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: In lua http filter add ability to set the…
steps.description:
Commit Message: This patch adds `setBytes()` API to the buffer wrapper by making the wrapped buffer to be mutable. This allows rewriting upstream data in the response path.

Risk Level: Low
Testing: Added unit and integration tests.
Docs Changes: Added
Release Notes: Added
```yaml
filter_chains:
- filters:
  - name: envoy.http_connection_manager
    config:
      http_filters:
        - name: envoy.lua
          config:
            inline_code: |
              function envoy_on_response(response_handle)
                if response_handle:headers():get(":status") != "200" then
                  response_handle:body():set('[]')
                end
              end
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: In lua http filter add ability to set the…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Commit Message: This patch adds `setBytes()` API to the buffer wrapper by making the wrapped buffer to be mutable. This allows rewriting upstream data in the response path.

Risk Level: Low
Testing: Added unit and integration tests.
Docs Changes: Added
Release Notes: Added
codeSnippets:
filter_chains:
- filters:
  - name: envoy.http_connection_manager
    config:
      http_filters:
        - name: envoy.lua
          config:
            inline_code: |
              function envoy_on_response(response_handle)
                if response_handle:headers():get(":status") != "200" then
                  response_handle:body():set('[]')
                end
              end

- name: envoy.filters.http.lua
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.filter.http.lua.v2.Lua
              inline_code: |
                function envoy_on_request(request_handle)
                end
                function envoy_on_response(response_handle)
                  response_handle:headers():replace("content-length", 6)
                  response_handle:body():set("hello\n")
                end

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
